en.name: Oxygen saturation (SpO2)
en.shortDescription: Oxygen saturation (SpO2) — the percentage of haemoglobin carrying oxygen, measured non-invasively by pulse oximetry at the periphery; reported in %.
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Oxygen saturation (SpO2) is the percentage of haemoglobin in the blood that is bound to oxygen, used to detect hypoxaemia (low blood oxygen). The "p" in SpO2 denotes **p**eripheral measurement by **p**ulse oximetry: a small optical sensor clipped to a finger, toe or earlobe shines light through the tissue and estimates arterial saturation non-invasively. Measuring it produces a percentage value (typically 95–100% on room air in a healthy adult) [1]. SpO2 is one of the core vital signs, displayed continuously on virtually every bedside and ward monitor and on portable oximeters. This concept set captures **non-invasive pulse-oximetry oxygen saturation values (SpO2)**. It is distinct from blood-gas oxygen saturation (SaO2) measured by co-oximetry on an arterial blood sample, which is a laboratory measurement covered elsewhere. It excludes interpretive findings (e.g., "oxygen saturation decreased") and derived or goal values (baseline, maximum, minimum, target SpO2). ## Included Concepts This concept set contains 20 standard concepts (LOINC + SNOMED). The SNOMED side is anchored on *4196147 / 431314004 — Peripheral oxygen saturation (SNOMED)* with descendants; the LOINC side is a curated list of pulse-oximetry concepts. The default for an unqualified SpO2 value is *40762499 / 59408-5 — Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry (LOINC)* or *4196147 / 431314004 — Peripheral oxygen saturation (SNOMED)*. Note: the LOINC concepts are named "Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry" — the "Arterial blood" wording reflects that pulse oximetry *estimates* arterial saturation; these are SpO2 (pulse-oximeter) values, not blood-gas measurements. ### Generic SpO2 - *40762499 / 59408-5 — Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry (LOINC)*: The generic SpO2 value, no context specified. Default mapping target. - *4196147 / 431314004 — Peripheral oxygen saturation (SNOMED)*: SNOMED equivalent of the generic peripheral (pulse-oximetry) oxygen saturation. ### By oxygen / rest context These specify whether the patient was on room air or supplemental oxygen, at rest or on exertion. - *40762501 / 59410-1 — Oxygen saturation ... --on room air (LOINC)*: SpO2 breathing room air (no supplemental oxygen). - *40762508 / 59417-6 — Oxygen saturation ... --resting (LOINC)*: SpO2 at rest. - *44810014 / 866661000000106 — Peripheral blood oxygen saturation on room air at rest (SNOMED)*, *44810015 / 866681000000102 — ... on room air on exertion (SNOMED)*, *44810016 / 866701000000100 — ... on supplemental oxygen at rest (SNOMED)*, *44810017 / 866721000000109 — ... on supplemental oxygen on exertion (SNOMED)*: SNOMED concepts combining oxygen source (room air vs. supplemental) with activity (rest vs. exertion); used in walk tests and respiratory assessments. ### Neonatal screening (pre- and postductal) - *40762498 / 59407-7 — Oxygen saturation in Blood Preductal by Pulse oximetry (LOINC)* and *40762509 / 59418-4 — Oxygen saturation in Blood Postductal by Pulse oximetry (LOINC)*: Pre- and postductal SpO2, taken at a right-hand and a foot site respectively, for neonatal critical congenital heart disease screening (a difference between the two suggests a ductus-dependent lesion). ### By clinical condition / treatment phase - *36305237 / 89276-0 — ... --W exercise (LOINC)* and *40762503 / 59412-7 — ... --post exercise (LOINC)*: SpO2 during and after exercise. - *36304254 / 89277-8 — ... --during anesthesia (LOINC)*: SpO2 under anaesthesia. - *40762500 / 59409-3 — ... --during treatment (LOINC)*, *40762504 / 59413-5 — ... --post treatment (LOINC)*, *40762507 / 59416-8 — ... --pre treatment (LOINC)*: SpO2 around a treatment. - *40762502 / 59411-9 — ... --post bronchodilation (LOINC)* and *40762505 / 59414-3 — ... --pre bronchodilation (LOINC)*: SpO2 before/after a bronchodilator. - *40762506 / 59415-0 — ... --pre physiotherapy (LOINC)*: SpO2 before chest physiotherapy. ## Excluded Concepts - *4095045 / 250553009 — Capillary oxygen saturation measurement (SNOMED, Procedure)*: Removed — this is the procedure (the act), not the resulting value. - *3661929 / 1321841000000106*, *3661930 / 1321861000000107*, *3661931 / 1321871000000100*, *3661932 / 1321881000000103 — Peripheral oxygen saturation ... on exertion decreased/unchanged (SNOMED, Clinical Finding)*: Removed — these are interpretive findings (the saturation went down / stayed the same), not numeric measurements. - Derived and goal values among the SNOMED descendants are excluded: *45770406 / 927981000000106 — Baseline SpO2*, *44813869 / 852651000000100 — Maximum peripheral oxygen saturation*, *44809213 / 852661000000102 — Minimum peripheral oxygen saturation*, *44809212 / 852641000000103 — Target peripheral oxygen saturation*. - Blood-gas oxygen saturation (SaO2) measured by co-oximetry on an arterial sample is not part of this set; it is a separate laboratory measurement. ## Mapping Notes - *Default concept*: When the source records an SpO2 / pulse-oximetry value with no context, map to Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry (40762499 / 59408-5, LOINC), or its SNOMED equivalent (4196147 / 431314004). Do not infer the oxygen source or activity level. - *SpO2 vs. SaO2*: This set is for the non-invasive pulse-oximeter value. If the source value comes from an arterial blood-gas analyser (co-oximetry), it is SaO2 and belongs to the blood-gas laboratory measurement, not here. - *Room air vs. supplemental oxygen*: Map to the room-air concept (40762501 / 59410-1, LOINC) only when the source states the patient was on room air. If oxygen status is not documented, use the generic concept. - *Neonatal screening*: Use the pre-/postductal concepts (40762498 / 59407-7 and 40762509 / 59418-4, LOINC) only in neonatal congenital-heart-disease screening, not for routine SpO2. - *Common source labels*: "SpO2", "SatO2", "Sat", "Pulse ox", "O2 sat", "Saturation" → Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry (40762499 / 59408-5, LOINC). - *Units*: SpO2 is reported as a percentage (%). ## References [1] Hafen BB, Sharma S. Oxygen Saturation. In: StatPearls. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2023. Available: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK525974/

fr.name: Saturation en oxygène (SpO2)
fr.shortDescription: Saturation en oxygène (SpO2) — le pourcentage d'hémoglobine transportant de l'oxygène, mesuré de façon non invasive par oxymétrie de pouls en périphérie ; en %.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Restrict to numeric pulse-oximetry SpO2 measurements. Removed 4 SNOMED clinical findings (oxygen saturation decreased/unchanged on exertion, 1321841000000106 etc.) and the SNOMED procedure Capillary oxygen saturation measurement (250553009). Anchor the SNOMED side on Peripheral oxygen saturation (431314004) with descendants, excluding derived/goal values (Baseline/Maximum/Minimum/Target peripheral oxygen saturation). Keep curated LOINC pulse-oximetry list. Added (SpO2) abbreviation and clinical description; moved to pending review.
